noveltyJustification: Recalibrates severity by separating default local stdio trust from governed/multi-tenant scope contracts.

ruledOut.approach: Keep the scope/auth findings as unconditional P0 under every deployment mode
ruledOut.reason: The runtime explicitly treats local stdio as a same-user trusted boundary and says access-scope enforcement is opt-in for multi-tenant deployments.

ruledOut.approach: Dismiss the findings because stdio is trusted
ruledOut.reason: The same system exposes governed scope fields and exact-match filters; once scope is supplied, community fallback and causal graph handlers bypass the promised boundary.

graphEvents.label: Should scope/auth P0 findings remain P0 under local single-user MCP threat model?
Scope/auth findings are conditional P0: P0 for governed/multi-tenant mode, P1 for default trusted local stdio.
causal graph tool schemas and handlers use bare IDs
